description_long: This is a headless marble statue depicting a draped human figure, likely from the Greco-Roman tradition. The figure is clothed in intricately carved, flowing robes that gather and fold naturally around the body, with detailed attention to the texture and weight of the fabric. The arms are partially visible beneath the drapery, with one arm bent and the other hanging by the side, both mostly obscured by the garment. The statue stands upright on a rectangular plinth, which serves as its base. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with the drapery falling in long, smooth folds. The overall surface is matte and slightly rough, typical of weathered or aged marble.
description: A headless marble statue of a draped human figure, standing on a rectangular plinth with detailed, flowing robes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed drapery and the headless upper torso, with the right arm partially visible beneath the folds.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the depth of the drapery and the way the fabric wraps around the figure's side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long, smooth folds of the robe with minimal anatomical detail.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the drapery's contour and the left arm's outline beneath the fabric.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
